extracted_codes.display: Occlusion and stenosis of carotid artery
extracted_codes.context: CAAD is documented in the EMR by ICD codes (i.e., ICD: 433.1)

extracted_codes.display: Occlusion and stenosis of carotid artery without mention of cerebral infarction
extracted_codes.context: Expand the ICD node below the Search button to see qualifying codes, which will be: 433.1, 433.10, 433.11

extracted_codes.display: Occlusion and stenosis of carotid artery with cerebral infarction
extracted_codes.context: Expand the ICD node below the Search button to see qualifying codes, which will be: 433.1, 433.10, 433.11
An exclusion criterion for the CAAD phenotype is hypercholesterolemia (ICD: 272.0)

extracted_codes.display: Duplex scan of extracranial arteries; complete bilateral study
extracted_codes.context: CAAD is measured quantitatively as a percent stenosis (narrowing) by Doppler ultrasound and related imaging studies (e.g., CPT: 93880)

clinical_criteria: Percent stenosis measurements from carotid imaging studies (extracted via NLP)
Contact on at least two (2) occasions ≥365 days apart in the preceding 5 years
Contact in at least three (3) different calendar quarters in the preceding 5 years
Contact in at least four (4) different calendar months in the preceding 5 years
Severe CAAD cases (specific stenosis thresholds not provided in documents)
Moderate CAAD cases (specific stenosis thresholds not provided in documents)
Measured controls (specific criteria not provided in documents)
Unmeasured controls (specific criteria not provided in documents)
algorithm_summary: The CAAD phenotype identifies carotid artery atherosclerosis disease cases and controls using both structured data and natural language processing. Cases are identified through ICD diagnosis codes (433.1*) and quantitative stenosis measurements extracted from imaging reports via NLP. The algorithm excludes patients with hypercholesterolemia (ICD 272.0). It defines two case categories (severe and moderate CAAD based on stenosis levels) and two control categories (measured and unmeasured). All patients must meet minimum healthcare contact requirements over 5 years. The portable NLP system extracts percent stenosis values from carotid ultrasound and other imaging study reports, which are then processed to determine patient-level stenosis classifications.
